skill.name: founder-os
skill.description: Helps founders organize venture portfolios, track business metrics, create strategic snapshots, and structure founder-led decisions.
skill.summary: Track founder portfolios, business signals, health snapshots, and strategic decision options.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, not executable Ruby or shell backticks. The weak cryptography and network reconnaissance findings are also keyword matches in descriptive business text. A medium risk remains because the skill instructs users to store, retrieve, share, and rotate sensitive credentials without enforceable security controls.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Credential Handling Without Enforced Controls
medium_findings.description: The skill tells users to save, retrieve, share, and rotate API keys, database passwords, AWS credentials, private keys, and certificates. It describes encryption, MFA, and audit logs, but the Markdown skill does not implement those controls or prevent secret disclosure in chat.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly reference API keys, database passwords, AWS credentials, private keys, and credential sharing. The risk is semantic rather than executable because this file contains guidance only.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified As External Commands
low_findings.description: The 25 external command findings are Markdown code block delimiters and example text. I found no shell command execution, Ruby backtick execution, or user input command interpolation in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is a Markdown code fence marker around examples. There is no executable source file or command runner in the skill.

low_findings.title: Keyword Matches Misclassified As Crypto Or Reconnaissance Risks
low_findings.description: The high and low blocker findings are descriptive text matches, not dangerous behavior. The file mentions AES-256, recovery codes, access logs, business registration, team design, and founder decisions, but it contains no weak algorithm implementation or network scanning logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are prose or command descriptions inside a Markdown file. I found no executable crypto code, weak cipher usage, port scanning, or host discovery behavior.

content.value_statement: Founders often track ventures, signals, and strategic choices across scattered tools. This skill provides a structured operating system for portfolio reviews, snapshots, and decision support.
actual_capabilities: Defines workflows for registering and updating business portfolio records.
Organizes revenue, growth, engagement, strategic, and health signals.
Outlines business health snapshots with SWOT analysis and recommended actions.
Provides founder-governed decision frameworks for hiring, expansion, and pivots.
Lists operational triggers for portfolio reviews, alerts, and monthly strategy reviews.
Describes credential vault workflows that require external secure systems.
limitations: It is a Markdown instruction file and does not implement storage, alerts, or encryption.
Credential handling requires a real vault service with audited security controls.
Financial forecasts depend on user-provided data and should be independently reviewed.
It does not provide real-time integrations with Stripe, competitors, surveys, or ML forecasting.

use_cases.title: Run Weekly Venture Reviews
use_cases.description: Compile metrics, surface risks, and define next actions for each active business.

use_cases.title: Prepare Strategic Decision Briefs
use_cases.description: Compare hiring, market expansion, fundraising, or pivot options with structured tradeoffs.

use_cases.title: Standardize Portfolio Reporting
use_cases.description: Create consistent snapshots for internal updates, advisor meetings, and investor discussions.

prompt_templates.title: Register A Business
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new venture
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me register a business in Founder OS. Ask for the minimum details needed, then summarize the profile.

prompt_templates.title: Track Monthly Signals
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a monthly signal update for my business using revenue, growth, engagement, team, and market metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Generate A Snapshot
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a business health snapshot with key metrics, SWOT analysis, top risks, opportunities, and recommended actions.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze A Strategic Choice
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze whether we should pursue this strategic option. Compare upside, cost, risk, timing, constraints, and a founder approval checklist.

output_examples.input: Show my business portfolio and identify which venture needs attention.
output_examples.output: A ranked portfolio review with health scores, recent signal changes, risk flags, and one recommended next step per venture.

output_examples.input: Should we hire a VP Sales this quarter?
output_examples.output: A decision brief comparing current runway, expected revenue impact, hiring cost, timing, alternatives, and approval questions.
An executive summary, metric trends, SWOT points, financial forecast, risks, opportunities, and next-month focus areas.

best_practices: Provide current metrics, dates, assumptions, and missing-data notes before asking for analysis.
Use a dedicated secret manager for credentials and avoid pasting raw secrets into chat.
Treat recommendations as decision support and keep final authority with the founder.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on the skill as a secure credential vault by itself.
Do not accept forecasts without checking source data and assumptions.
Do not automate strategic business changes without explicit founder approval.

faq.question: Does this skill store business data by itself?
faq.answer: No. It describes workflows and report structures. Storage requires a separate database or workspace system.

faq.question: Can it manage API keys securely?
faq.answer: Not by itself. Use a dedicated vault and avoid sending raw secrets to the assistant.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The generated report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is it suitable for investor updates?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure metric summaries, strategy reviews, and decision briefs for advisor or investor conversations.

faq.question: Does it make decisions for the founder?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that strategic decisions remain founder-governed and require approval.

faq.question: What is the main security caution?
faq.answer: Credential workflows are sensitive. Use external security controls, access logs, MFA, and secret rotation outside this skill.
